revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 練馬大鳥神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=練馬区}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[練馬区]][[豊玉北]]5-18-2 |地図 = |祭神 = [[天日鷲神|天之日鷲命]]、鶴霊神 |社格 = |創建 = [[正保]]年間（[[1645年]]～[[1648年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''練馬大鳥神社'''（ねりまおおとりじんじゃ）は[[東京都]][[練馬区]]の[[神社]]。
== 概要 == [[正保]]年間（[[1645年]]～[[1648年]]）に創建された。三羽の[[ツル|鶴]]が当地に飛来したので保護したものの、死んでしまったことから「霊鳥」として崇め、鳥に関係する[[大鳥大社]]から[[分霊]]を勧請して神社にしたのが由来である。
[[境内]]には、「石薬師堂」と呼ばれる[[薬師如来]]の祠がある。自然石に「薬師如来」と刻まれており、[[1883年]]（[[明治]]16年）に建立された<ref name="史跡散歩">江幡潤 著『練馬区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド20)』[[学生社]]、1993年、86-87p</ref>。
当社では、毎年11月に[[酉の市]]が開かれている<ref name="酉の市">[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ 酉の市とは]（有）シャープウェッジ</ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[練馬駅]]より徒歩1分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *江幡潤 著『練馬区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド20)』[[学生社]]、1993年
== 関連項目 == * [[酉の市]]
==外部リンク== *[http://torinoichi.info/ 練馬大鳥神社酉の市]
{{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ねりまおおとりしんしや}} [[Category:東京都区部の神社]] [[Category:練馬区の歴史]] [[Category:練馬区の建築物]]

[[境内]]には、「石薬師堂」と呼ばれる[[薬師如来]]の祠がある。自然石に「薬師如来」と刻まれており、[[1883年]]（[[明治]]16年）に建立された<ref name="史跡散歩">江幡潤 著『練馬区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド20)』[[学生社]]、1993年、86-87p</ref>。
当社では、毎年11月に[[酉の市]]が開かれている<ref name="酉の市">[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ 酉の市とは]（有）シャープウェッジ</ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[練馬駅]]より徒歩1分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *江幡潤 著『練馬区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド20)』[[学生社]]、1993年
== 関連項目 == * [[酉の市]]

練馬大鳥神社
[[Category:練馬大鳥神社]][[ja:練馬大鳥神社]]

{{Shrine in Japan |Name = Nerima Ootori Shrine |Image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |Deities = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神}}, Crane Spirit Deity |Shrine Rank = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ Nerima Ootori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] [[Category:Shrines in Nerima]] [[Category:練馬大鳥神社]][[ja:練馬大鳥神社]]

The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神}}, Crane Spirit Deity |Shrine Rank = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. {{Infobox shrine |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神}}, Crane Spirit Deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ Nerima Ootori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ Nerima Ootori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] [[Category:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Nerima Ōtori Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Nerima Ōtori Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Nerima Ōtori Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Shrines in Nerima]] [[Category:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Nerima Ōtori Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Nerima Ōtori Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Nerima Ōtori Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:練馬大鳥神社]][[Category:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Nerima Ōtori Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Nerima Ōtori Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]][[ja:練馬大鳥神社]]

Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ Nerima Ootori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] [[Category:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ Nerima Ootori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] [[Category:Nerima Ōtori {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. Station|ja|練馬駅}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ Nerima Ootori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] [[Category:Nerima Ōtori {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ Nerima Ootori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] [[Category:Nerima Ōtori religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nerima Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Shōhō Era|ja|正保}} (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the {{ill|Shōhō era|ja|正保}} (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}, related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}}. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. ''Nerima-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide Vol. 20).'' Gakuseisha, 1993. == See Also == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} * [http://torinoichi.info/ Tori-no-Ichi Official Site] {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima Ward]] [[ja:%E7%B7%B4%E9%A6%AC%E5%A4%A7%E9%B3%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]][[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nerima Ootori Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nerima Ootori Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Shōhō Era|ja|正保}} (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the {{ill|Shōhō era|ja|正保}} (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}, related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}}. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Shōhō Era|ja|正保}} (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the {{ill|Shōhō era|ja|正保}} (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}, related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}}. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. ''Nerima-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide Vol. 20).'' Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Shōhō Era|ja|正保}} (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the {{ill|Shōhō era|ja|正保}} (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}, related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}}. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Stone Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Enbata, Jun. ''Nerima-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide Vol. 20).'' {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nerima Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Shōhō Era|ja|正保}} (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the {{ill|Shōhō era|ja|正保}} (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}, related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}}. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Stone Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Enbata, Jun. ''Nerima-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide Vol. 20).'' Gakuseisha, 1993. == See Also == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} * [http://torinoichi.info/ Tori-no-Ichi Official Site] {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima Ward]] [[ja:%E7%B7%B4%E9%A6%AC%E5%A4%A7%E9%B3%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]][[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nerima Ootori Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nerima Ootori Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nerima Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Shōhō Era|ja|正保}} (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the {{ill|Shōhō era|ja|正保}} (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}, related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}}. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Stone Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Enbata, Jun. ''Nerima-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide Vol. 20).'' Gakuseisha, 1993. == See Also == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} * [http://torinoichi.info/ Tori-no-Ichi Official Site] {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima Ward]] [[ja:%E7%B7%B4%E9%A6%AC%E5%A4%A7%E9%B3%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]][[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nerima Ootori Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nerima Ootori Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. Torii and Worship Hall

The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来}}. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区|12=draft}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|12=draft}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区|12=draft}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha|12=draft}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来|12=draft}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ Nerima Ootori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nerima Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区|12=draft}}, Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命|12=draft}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Shōhō Era|ja|正保|12=draft}} (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the {{ill|Shōhō era|ja|正保|12=draft}} (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴|12=draft}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha|12=draft}}, related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来|12=draft}}. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. ''Nerima-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide Vol. 20).'' {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区|12=draft}}, Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命|12=draft}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Shōhō Era|ja|正保|12=draft}} (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the {{ill|Shōhō era|ja|正保|12=draft}} (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴|12=draft}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha|12=draft}}, related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来|12=draft}}. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Stone Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅|12=draft}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Enbata, Jun. ''Nerima-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide Vol. 20).'' {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区|12=draft}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|12=draft}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区|12=draft}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha|12=draft}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来|12=draft}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区|12=draft|12=draft}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|12=draft|12=draft}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai (Me)dicine Buddha|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft|12=draft}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ Nerima Ootori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nerima Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区|12=draft|12=draft}}, Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Shōhō Era|ja|正保|12=draft|12=draft}} (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the {{ill|Shōhō era|ja|正保|12=draft|12=draft}} (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴|12=draft|12=draft}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai (Me)dicine Buddha|12=draft|12=draft}}. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft|12=draft}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Stone Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Enbata, Jun. ''Nerima-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide Vol. 20).'' Gakuseisha, 1993. == See Also == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} * [http://torinoichi.info/ Tori-no-Ichi Official Site] {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Nerima]] [[Category:練馬大鳥神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima Ward]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] }} [[sn:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]] [[ja:練馬大鳥神社]] [[en:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%B7%B4%E9%A6%AC%E5%A4%A7%E9%B3%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nerima Ootori Shrine]]

revision.comment: 4 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:練馬大鳥神社 up to rev 101823639
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 練馬大鳥神社 |画像 = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[練馬区]][[豊玉北]]5-18-2 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分=44|緯度秒=12.8 |経度度 = 139|経度分=39|経度秒=15 |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Tokyo city |祭神 = [[天日鷲神|天之日鷲命]]、鶴霊神 |社格 = |創建 = [[正保]]年間（[[1645年]]～[[1648年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''練馬大鳥神社'''（ねりまおおとりじんじゃ）は[[東京都]][[練馬区]]の[[神社]]。 == 概要 == [[正保]]年間（[[1645年]]～[[1648年]]）に創建された。三羽の[[ツル|鶴]]が当地に飛来したので保護したものの、死んでしまったことから「霊鳥」として崇め、鳥に関係する[[大鳥大社]]から[[分霊]]を勧請して神社にしたのが由来である。 [[境内]]には、「石薬師堂」と呼ばれる[[薬師如来]]の祠がある。自然石に「薬師如来」と刻まれており、[[1883年]]（[[明治]]16年）に建立された江幡潤 著『練馬区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド20)』[[学生社]]、1993年、86-87p。 当社では、毎年11月に[[酉の市]]が開かれている[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ 酉の市とは]（有）シャープウェッジ。 File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|拝殿 File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|石薬師如来 == 交通アクセス == *[[練馬駅]]より徒歩1分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *江幡潤 著『練馬区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド20)』[[学生社]]、1993年 == 関連項目 == * [[酉の市]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ 練馬大鳥神社酉の市] {{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ねりまおおとりしんしや}} [[Category:練馬区の神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 練馬大鳥神社 |画像 = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[練馬区]][[豊玉北]]5-18-2 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分=44|緯度秒=12.8 |経度度 = 139|経度分=39|経度秒=15 |ラベル位置 = top |地図 = Tokyo city |祭神 = [[天日鷲神|天之日鷲命]]、鶴霊神 |社格 = |創建 = [[正保]]年間（[[1645年]]～[[1648年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''練馬大鳥神社'''（ねりまおおとりじんじゃ）は[[東京都]][[練馬区]]の[[神社]]。 == 概要 == [[正保]]年間（[[1645年]]～[[1648年]]）に創建された。三羽の[[ツル|鶴]]が当地に飛来したので保護したものの、死んでしまったことから「霊鳥」として崇め、鳥に関係する[[大鳥大社]]から[[分霊]]を勧請して神社にしたのが由来である。 [[境内]]には、「石薬師堂」と呼ばれる[[薬師如来]]の祠がある。自然石に「薬師如来」と刻まれており、[[1883年]]（[[明治]]16年）に建立された江幡潤 著『練馬区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド20)』[[学生社]]、1993年、86-87p。 当社では、毎年11月に[[酉の市]]が開かれている[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ 酉の市とは]（有）シャープウェッジ。 File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|拝殿 File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|石薬師如来 == 交通アクセス == *[[練馬駅]]より徒歩1分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *江幡潤 著『練馬区史跡散歩 (東京史跡ガイド20)』[[学生社]]、1993年 == 関連項目 == * [[酉の市]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ 練馬大鳥神社酉の市] {{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ねりまおおとりしんしや}} [[Category:練馬区の神社]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai (Me)dicine Buddha}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ Nerima Ootori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nerima Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Shōhō Era|ja|正保}} (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the {{ill|Shōhō era|ja|正保}} (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}, related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai (Me)dicine Buddha}}. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Stone Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|ja|練馬駅}}. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Enbata, Jun. ''Nerima-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide Vol. 20).'' Gakuseisha, 1993. == See Also == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} * [http://torinoichi.info/ Tori-no-Ichi Official Site] {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Nerima]] [[Category:練馬大鳥神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima Ward]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] }} [[sn:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]] [[ja:練馬大鳥神社]] [[en:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%B7%B4%E9%A6%AC%E5%A4%A7%E9%B3%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nerima Ootori Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|練馬大鳥神社|version=101823639|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区|qq=draft}}, Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Shōhō Era|ja|正保|qq=draft}} (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the {{ill|Shōhō era|ja|正保|qq=draft}} (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴|qq=draft}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha|qq=draft}}, related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai (Me)dicine Buddha|qq=draft}}. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区|qq=draft}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|qq=draft}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区|qq=draft}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha|qq=draft}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai (Me)dicine Buddha|qq=draft}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co..
