{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerima Ward|qq=}}, Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Shōhō Era|ja|正保|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō Era|qq=}} (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerima Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the {{ill|Shōhō era|ja|正保|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō era|qq=}} (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cranes|qq=}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori taisha|qq=}}, related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi Nyorai|13=User:Immanuelle/Bhaisajyaguru|qq=}}. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Nerima|ja|練馬区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerima|qq=}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nerima, Tokyo|ja|練馬区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nerima, Tokyo|qq=}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori taisha|qq=}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Yakushi Nyorai|ja|薬師如来|1=Bhaisajyaguru|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushi Nyorai|13=User:Immanuelle/Bhaisajyaguru|qq=}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima]], Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nerima|Nerima, Tokyo]], Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (the Buddha of Healing). {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima]], Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nerima|Nerima, Tokyo]], Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from [[:en:Nerima Station|Nerima Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ Nerima Ootori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nerima Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō Era]] (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō era]] (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cranes|qq=}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Stone Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * 1-minute walk from [[:en:Nerima Station|Nerima Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Enbata, Jun. ''Nerima-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide Vol. 20).'' Gakuseisha, 1993. == See Also == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} * [http://torinoichi.info/ Tori-no-Ichi Official Site] {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|練馬大鳥神社|version=101823639|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Nerima]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima Ward]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] }} [[sn:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]] [[ja:練馬大鳥神社]] [[ja:%E7%B7%B4%E9%A6%AC%E5%A4%A7%E9%B3%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nerima Ootori Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Nerima Otori-jinja}}

After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], related to birds. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q97189034]] Wikidata name: Nerima Ōtori Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima]], Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = |prefecture = {{ill|Toyotama-Kita|qid=Q11633962}}|coordinates = {{Coord|35.736889|N|139.65417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nerima|Nerima, Tokyo]], Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from [[:en:Nerima Station|Nerima Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ Nerima Ootori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nerima Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō Era]] (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō era]] (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cranes|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツル}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Stone Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * 1-minute walk from [[:en:Nerima Station|Nerima Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Enbata, Jun. ''Nerima-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide Vol. 20).'' Gakuseisha, 1993. == See Also == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} * [http://torinoichi.info/ Tori-no-Ichi Official Site] {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|練馬大鳥神社|version=101823639|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]] [[ja:練馬大鳥神社]] [[ja:%E7%B7%B4%E9%A6%AC%E5%A4%A7%E9%B3%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nerima Ootori Shrine]] {{Commons category|Nerima Otori-jinja}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q97189034]] Wikidata name: Nerima Ōtori Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima]], Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Toyotama-Kita|qid=Q11633962}}|coordinates = {{Coord|35.736889|N|139.65417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nerima|Nerima, Tokyo]], Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from [[:en:Nerima Station|Nerima Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ Nerima Ootori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nerima Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō Era]] (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō era]] (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cranes|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツル|1=Gruidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25365}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Stone Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * 1-minute walk from [[:en:Nerima Station|Nerima Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Enbata, Jun. ''Nerima-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide Vol. 20).'' Gakuseisha, 1993. == See Also == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} * [http://torinoichi.info/ Tori-no-Ichi Official Site] {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|練馬大鳥神社|version=101823639|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nerima Ōtori Shrine]] [[ja:練馬大鳥神社]] [[ja:%E7%B7%B4%E9%A6%AC%E5%A4%A7%E9%B3%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nerima Ootori Shrine]] {{Commons category|Nerima Otori-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q97189034}}

Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cranes|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツル|1=Gruidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25365}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], related to birds. [[sn:commons:Category:Nerima Otori-jinja]][[ja:練馬大鳥神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q97189034]] Wikidata name: Nerima Ōtori Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima]], Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Toyotama-Kita|WD=Q11633962}}|coordinates = {{Coord|35.736889|N|139.65417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nerima|Nerima, Tokyo]], Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. Torii and Worship Hall

Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cranes|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツル|1=Gruidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25365}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. ''Nerima-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide Vol. 20).'' After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Stone Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * 1-minute walk from [[:en:Nerima Station|Nerima Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Enbata, Jun. ''Nerima-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide Vol. 20).'' Gakuseisha, 1993. == See Also == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} * [http://torinoichi.info/ Tori-no-Ichi Official Site] {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|練馬大鳥神社|version=101823639|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:練馬大鳥神社]] [[ja:%E7%B7%B4%E9%A6%AC%E5%A4%A7%E9%B3%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{Commons category|Nerima Otori-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q97189034}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Nerima Otori-jinja]][[ja:練馬大鳥神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97189034]] Wikidata name: Nerima Ōtori Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima]], Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Toyotama-Kita|WD=Q11633962}}|coordinates = {{Coord|35.736889|N|139.65417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nerima|Nerima, Tokyo]], Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from [[:en:Nerima Station|Nerima Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ Nerima Ootori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nerima Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō Era]] (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō era]] (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cranes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツル|1=Gruidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25365}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Stone Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * 1-minute walk from [[:en:Nerima Station|Nerima Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Enbata, Jun. ''Nerima-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide Vol. 20).'' Gakuseisha, 1993. == See Also == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} * [http://torinoichi.info/ Tori-no-Ichi Official Site] {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|練馬大鳥神社|version=101823639|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:練馬大鳥神社]] [[ja:%E7%B7%B4%E9%A6%AC%E5%A4%A7%E9%B3%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{Commons category|Nerima Otori-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q97189034}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Nerima Otori-jinja]][[ja:練馬大鳥神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97189034]] Wikidata name: Nerima Ōtori Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima]], Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Toyotama-Kita|WD=Q11633962}}|coordinates = {{Coord|35.736889|N|139.65417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nerima|Nerima, Tokyo]], Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from [[:en:Nerima Station|Nerima Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ Nerima Ootori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nerima Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō Era]] (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō era]] (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cranes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツル|1=Gruidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25365}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from [[:en:Nerima Station|Nerima Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ Nerima Ootori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nerima Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō Era]] (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō era]] (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cranes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツル|1=Gruidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25365}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Stone Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * 1-minute walk from [[:en:Nerima Station|Nerima Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Enbata, Jun. ''Nerima-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide Vol. 20).'' Gakuseisha, 1993. == See Also == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} * [http://torinoichi.info/ Tori-no-Ichi Official Site] {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|練馬大鳥神社|version=101823639|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:練馬大鳥神社]] [[ja:%E7%B7%B4%E9%A6%AC%E5%A4%A7%E9%B3%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{Commons category|Nerima Otori-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q97189034}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[commons:Category:Nerima Otori-jinja]][[ja:練馬大鳥神社]] [[commons:Category:Nerima Otori-jinja]][[ja:練馬大鳥神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nerima Otori-jinja]][[ja:練馬大鳥神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97189034]] Wikidata name: Nerima Ōtori Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima]], Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Toyotama-Kita|WD=Q11633962}}|coordinates = {{Coord|35.736889|N|139.65417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nerima|Nerima, Tokyo]], Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]] (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from [[:en:Nerima Station|Nerima Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ Nerima Ootori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nerima Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō Era]] (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō era]] (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cranes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツル|1=Gruidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25365}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Stone Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * 1-minute walk from [[:en:Nerima Station|Nerima Station]]. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * Enbata, Jun. ''Nerima-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide Vol. 20).'' Gakuseisha, 1993. == See Also == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} * [http://torinoichi.info/ Tori-no-Ichi Official Site] {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|練馬大鳥神社|version=101823639|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:練馬大鳥神社]] [[ja:%E7%B7%B4%E9%A6%AC%E5%A4%A7%E9%B3%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{Commons category|Nerima Otori-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q97189034}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], a shrine associated with birds. The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from [[:en:Nerima Station|Nerima Station]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ Nerima Ootori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nerima Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō Era]] (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the [[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō era]] (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cranes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツル|1=Gruidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25365}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]], related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]]. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}. ''Nerima-ku Historical Walk (Tokyo Historical Guide Vol. 20).'' Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cranes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツル|1=Gruidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25365}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori taisha}}, related to birds. [[co:commons:Category:Nerima Otori-jinja]][[ja:練馬大鳥神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Nerima Otori-jinja]][[ja:練馬大鳥神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nerima Otori-jinja]][[ja:練馬大鳥神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg |location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Nerima|en|Nerima|WD=Q232655|lt=Nerima}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Toyotama-Kita|WD=Q11633962}}|coordinates = {{Coord|35.736889|N|139.65417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nerima|en|Nerima|WD=Q232655|lt=Nerima, Tokyo}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori taisha}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori taisha|1=Ōtori taisha}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha|1=Bhaisajyaguru}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori taisha|1=Ōtori taisha}}, a shrine associated with birds. The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Nerima Ootori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg |location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Nerima|en|Nerima|WD=Q232655|lt=Nerima|1=Nerima}}, Toyotamakita 5-18-2 |Latitude = 35|LatMinutes=44|LatSeconds=12.8 |Longitude = 139|LonMinutes=39|LonSeconds=15 |Label Position = top |Map = Tokyo city |deity = {{ill|Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, Crane Spirit deity |type = |Established = Shōhō Era (1645–1648) |Main Hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage Stations = |Festivals = |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Toyotama-Kita|WD=Q11633962|1=Toyotama-Kita}}|coordinates = {{Coord|35.736889|N|139.65417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Nerima Ootori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nerima|en|Nerima|WD=Q232655|lt=Nerima, Tokyo|1=Nerima}}, Japan. == Overview == Nerima Ootori Shrine was established during the Shōhō era (1645–1648). The shrine's origin story recounts the arrival of three cranes to the area. Although they were protected, they eventually died, and were subsequently revered as "sacred birds." The shrine was founded by enshrining a portion of the spirit (''bunrei'') from {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori taisha|1=Ōtori taisha}}, a shrine associated with birds. Within the precincts is a small hall called the "Ishi Yakushi-dō," dedicated to {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha|1=Bhaisajyaguru}} (the Buddha of Healing). The natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai" was erected in 1883 (Meiji 16)Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993, pp. 86-87. Each November, the shrine hosts the {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} festival[http://torinoichi.info/torinoichi/ What is Tori-no-Ichi?] by Sharp Wedge Co.. File:Nerima Otori-jinja hall of worship 2023-04-27.jpg|Worship Hall File:Nerima Otori-jinja Ishi-yakushi-nyorai 2023-01-03.jpg|Ishi Yakushi Nyorai == Access == * A 1-minute walk from {{ill|Nerima Station|en|Nerima Station|WD=Q134373840|lt=Nerima Station|1=Nerima Station}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Jun Ebata, ''Nerima-ku Historic Walk (Tokyo Historic Guide 20),'' Gakuseisha, 1993 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nerima Otori-jinja}} *[http://torinoichi.info/ Nerima Ootori Shrine Tori-no-Ichi Festival] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nerima Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nerima Otori-jinja entrance 2023-04-27.jpg|250px]]
Torii and Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|44|12.8|N|139|39|15|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Nerima|en|Nerima|WD=Q232655|lt=Nerima Ward|1=Nerima}}, Toyotama Kita 5-18-2 | Latitude = 35| Latitude_min=44| Latitude_sec=12.8 | Longitude = 139| Longitude_min=39| Longitude_sec=15 | Label_Position = top | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhiwashi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}}, Tsurei-shin (Spirit of the Crane) | Historical Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Shōhō|en|Shōhō|WD=Q1146192|lt=Shōhō Era|1=Shōhō}} (1645–1648) | Main Building = | Alternate name = | Sacred Sites = | Festival = | Rituals = }} '''Nerima Ōtori Shrine''' (練馬大鳥神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nerima|en|Nerima|WD=Q232655|lt=Nerima Ward|1=Nerima}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. == Overview == The shrine was founded during the {{ill|Shōhō|en|Shōhō|WD=Q1146192|lt=Shōhō era|1=Shōhō}} (1645–1648). Its origins trace back to the arrival of three {{ill|cranes|ja|鶴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cranes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツル|1=Crane (bird)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q25365}} in the area. After their passing, they were revered as "sacred birds," leading to the enshrinement of deities from {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori taisha|1=Ōtori taisha}}, related to birds. The shrine grounds include the "Stone Yakushi Hall," a small sanctuary for the {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|WD=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha|1=Bhaisajyaguru}}. The sanctuary, made from natural stone engraved with "Yakushi Nyorai," was established in 1883{{Sfn|Nerima Historical Walk|1993|pp=86–87}}. Each November, the shrine hosts the annual {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, a festival dedicated to good fortune and prosperity{{Sfn|What is Tori-no-Ichi?|Sharp Wedge Co.|}}.
